purpose: Verify that PHI/liveness analysis correctly tracks loop-carried variables (sum and i) across a simple while-loop back-edge; sum must accumulate 0+1+...+9=45.

source.text: int main(void) { int i = 0; int sum = 0; while (i < 10) { sum = sum + i; i = i + 1; } return sum; }

pass_condition: target exit code equals 45 (same as reference cc)

purpose: Verify parallel copy semantics for swapping two variables inside a loop; after 10 (even) swaps a must return to its original value 12.
pass_condition: target exit code equals 12 (10 swaps is even, a returns to original value)

purpose: Verify PHI/liveness across nested while-loops; outer 5 iterations each containing 3 inner iterations adding 2 gives sum=30.

source.text: int main(void) { int i = 0; int sum = 0; while (i < 5) { int j = 0; while (j < 3) { sum = sum + 2; j = j + 1; } i = i + 1; } return sum; }

purpose: Verify liveness of a variable used on a loop back-edge where result (2037) would wrap modulo 256 as Unix exit status; use stdout oracle instead of exit_code.

source.text: int printf(char*, ...); int main(void) { int x = 1; int i = 0; while(i < 10) { int y = x * 2; x = y + i; i++; } printf("%d\n", x); return 0; }

pass_condition: target exit code 0 and stdout '2037\n' (exit_code 2037 wraps mod 256, stdout is authoritative)

purpose: Verify PHI completeness when a loop body contains if/else branches that modify the same variable; result after 8 iterations of alternating add/multiply is 108.

source.text: int main(void) { int x = 0; int i=0; while(i<8){ if(i%2==0){ x = x + i; } else { x = x * 3; } i++; } return x; }

purpose: Verify that variables live across a function call boundary are correctly spilled/restored; callee(x)*2+i iterated 5 times starting from x=1 yields 58.

source.text: int callee(int x) { return x*2; } int main(void) { int x=1; for(int i=0;i<5;i++){ x = callee(x) + i; } return x; }

purpose: Verify PHI node ordering under dominance: unconditional if(1) sets x=10, dead else branch sets x=20, then while(x>0) decrements to 0; return must be 0.

source.text: int main(void) { int x=0; if(1){ x=10; } else { x=20; } while(x>0){ x--; } return x; }

purpose: Verify PHI/liveness across recursive calls; fact(6)=720 would wrap as a Unix exit status so stdout is the authoritative oracle.

source.text: int printf(char*, ...); int fact(int n) { if (n <= 1) return 1; return n * fact(n-1); } int main(void) { printf("%d\n", fact(6)); return 0; }

purpose: Verify parallel return values from recursive calls are correctly merged; fib(8)=21 via binary recursion.
Verify accumulator stays live across recursive call boundary; rec(10,0) computes 1+2+...+10=55.

source.text: int rec(int n, int acc) { if (n == 0) return acc; return rec(n-1, acc + n); } int main(void) { return rec(10, 0); }

purpose: Verify PHI/liveness when recursion interacts with a loop inside the recursive function; rec(5) sums triangular numbers giving 20.

source.text: int rec(int n) { int sum = 0; for(int i=0; i<n; i++) sum += i; if (n > 0) return sum + rec(n-1); return sum; } int main(void) { return rec(5); }

purpose: Verify PHI/liveness across mutually recursive functions; even(10) with even/odd mutual recursion returns 1.

source.text: int even(int n); int odd(int n) { if (n == 0) return 0; return even(n-1); } int even(int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; return odd(n-1); } int main(void) { return even(10); }

purpose: Verify tail-like recursive accumulator; rec(15,0) computes 1+2+...+15=120.

source.text: int rec(int n, int acc) { if (n == 0) return acc; return rec(n-1, acc + n); } int main(void) { return rec(15, 0); }

purpose: Verify PHI/liveness in deep conditional recursion with two different recursive step sizes; rec(12) with even/odd branching yields 84.

source.text: int rec(int n) { if (n <= 0) return 0; if (n % 2 == 0) return rec(n-1) + n; else return rec(n-2) + n*2; } int main(void) { return rec(12); }
